expectations.text: Notes strict priority prefers the smaller (constant -> scalar) step first (the coach's own framing, not just an echo of the prompt token)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: 'TPP ranks transformations simplest-first, and (scalar -> array) sits strictly below (constant -> scalar). The rule is: apply the highest-priority transformation that makes the current red test pass.'

expectations.text: ALLOWS the reasoned deviation to (scalar -> array) as a heuristic with a stated reason -- does NOT rigidly refuse ('no, take the higher-priority move')
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Treats priority order as a hard rule: 'array is off the table by definition'; 'You only drop to a lower-priority one when no simpler transformation can turn this test green.' No acknowledgment that a reasoned deviation is legitimately allowable; redirects foresight to test ordering only.

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
